compositional_truth_check.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears barren.
compositional_truth_check.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears barren.
compositional_truth_check.p3_black_pot: Systemic Loss: White rabbit anchor remains, but primary Pothos specimen is absent/lost.
compositional_truth_check.p4_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: All registered specimens (P1-P4) have suffered total systemic loss. The desk surface now contains only inert objects (rabbit, eggshells, electronic components).

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and devoid of organic life.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments and electronic component (multi-pin connector) on the desk surface.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single green leaf fragment in the black pot; rapid decline observed.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_current: Progressive disappearance of all plant matter. By T-2 and CURRENT, the pots are entirely empty, indicating complete senescence or removal of specimens.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: Total mortality of the biome. The environment is currently non-viable for the registered species.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue, absence of turgor pressure, and complete loss of structural integrity in all pots.

anomalies: Introduction of electronic multi-pin connector on the desk surface.
Persistent presence of eggshell fragments as a non-botanical additive.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Starting from the earliest image, which contained minimal vegetative remnants, the biome has transitioned to a state of complete vacancy. The pots are now devoid of their registered occupants. The presence of non-organic debris (eggshells, electronics) suggests the area is no longer being maintained as a functional botanical environment.
